# Functions and Graphs

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this chapter, you will be able to:

- Determine whether a relationship is a function from a graph, table, or equation.
- Identify the domain and range of a function in context.
- Read and interpret transformations of a graph.
- Build and evaluate piecewise (multipart) functions.

---

## Function Definition & Notation

*(What makes something a function; function notation review.)*

## Domain & Range

*(Determining domain/range from context — e.g. a population function restricted to a season.)*

### Concept Check: Function vs. Non-Function {-}

*(To be drafted.)*

## Graph Reading & Transformations (Introduction)

*(Reading key features off a graph; a first look at shifts, ahead of Chapter 6.)*

## Multipart (Piecewise) Functions

*(Tiered water-rate pricing or emissions-penalty schedules as piecewise functions.)*

### Concept Check: Evaluating Piecewise Functions {-}

*(To be drafted.)*

## Practice: Build a Piecewise Model

*(Building a piecewise model for a tiered utility/policy structure.)*

## Skills Drills

### Evaluating Functions

*(To be drafted.)*

### Sketching From a Table

*(To be drafted.)*
